A public photo and report submitted to KIFI Local News 8 indicates a small tornado touched down just northwest of the St Anthony Sand Dunes...viewed looking northeast from the Hamer area. Time and exact location are estimated based on the report and radar. No damage reported. Tornado was likely brief and weak. Aerial imagery suggests the tornado occurred in an open area of grass and juniper with limited access. Unless additional reports are received...tornado will be rated an EFU.

Blowing dust and ash from the recent Lava Trail Fire reduced visibility causing a shut down of HWY 20 for over 12 hours until Friday morning. Winds in the area were gusting around 35-45 mph Thursday evening. The HWY is now open after winds have decreased.

Two large carports destroyed and blown in to a wooden fence, knocking the fence down. Also, a trampoline lofted landing on a utility trailer in the side of a residential yard. Several large tree limbs knocked off of a tree in the area. Only isolated damage noted at one or two houses in the neighborhood. Video of the incident showed a brief microburst focused in this location. Report relayed via media and time estimated via radar.

On 10/27/25 at approximately 1:45 to 1:50 PM MDT, a brief EF0 tornado occurred near Rigby, ID. Multiple initial reports of a funnel cloud were observed on social media followed after by reports of damage isolated to one property along N 3950 E. A damage survey revealed tree and property damage consistent with 75-85 mph winds including two large uprooted pine trees, a recreation vehicle flipped on its side, a damaged outbuilding, and a destroyed partially constructed sunroom attachment on the residence. The tornado path was approximately 200 yards long, with a maximum width of about 50 yards. The tornado was on the ground for approximately five minutes with time of the report assisted by radar and video taken at the residence.
